metadata.maturity_notes: Lite diagnostic variant; inherits the parent maturity tier but should be evaluated separately because it has smaller scope and less bespoke hook coverage.
metadata.challenge_summary: Each agent's local action can worsen the whole team's global alert state, creating a sequential social dilemma.
dilemmas: Ringing the bell can reveal information but raises danger
Fast combat may be noisier than avoidance
One greedy action can impose costs on both heroes
warden_runbook: Escalate only through visible bell/alert thresholds
metadata.lite_focus: A compact bell room with one cultist and one rat-pack alarm runner; agents must intercept without raising noise.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: coordinate quiet actions before bell pressure escalates
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: pressure the declared MARL axis: shared global externality management
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: coordinate extraction once the objective is secured

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into Bells Under Blackwater is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

middle_room.name: Middle Room
middle_room.description: A compact pressure room isolates the challenge: shared global externality management.

objective_room.name: Objective Room
objective_room.description: The core object of the lite challenge is visible here; taking it should trigger extraction thinking, not exhaustive exploration.

lower_route.name: Lower Route
lower_route.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Drowned Bell
furniture.description: Primary clue object for shared global externality management. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains shared global externality management.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: A compact bell room with one cultist and one rat-pack alarm runner; agents must intercept without raising noise.

core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: Escalate only through visible bell/alert thresholds
pressure the declared MARL axis: shared global externality management
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.
